title: Upstart (UPST) to Report Q3 Earnings: What's in the Offing?
text: For the third quarter of 2022, Upstart expected revenue of $170 million, with the Zacks Consensus Estimate for the top line pegged at $171.67 million, suggesting a decline of 24.86% from the year-ago reported figure. Key Business Drivers: Upstart's acquisition of Prodigy in 2021 was likely to have reaped benefits for the company in the third quarter, as Prodigy helped Upstart roll out its auto retail products. Due to the buyout, Upstart's dealership partnership extended from 500 dealers in the first quarter 2022 to 640 in the last reported quarter. Upstart's third-quarter performance was likely to have benefited from its strategy to operate as a multi-product company, with strength across Upstart's auto refinance and personal loan product portfolios expected to have favored the company's performance. Headwinds: The company reported a loss of $30.183 million in the net interest income segment in the second quarter of 2022. UPST entered multiple loan sale transactions negatively impacted by the rising interest rate environment, which was expected to have continued in the third quarter, increasing loan pricing and lowering approval rates, thus reducing transaction volume and affecting the top line.
